info.title: Exact Horeko API
info.description: Welcome to the Horeko API. To get started using the API, you first need to login. Our API is following the OAuth 2.0 standard for authentication, and it supports only HTTPS connections. Your application (from here on referred to as: the client), should use the following route: https://api.horeko.com/account/login Because this is a POST request, the client should send a model as body containing the following properties: 1. username (your username for the Horeko application) 2. password (your password for the Horeko application) 3. grant_type (type of the grant, hardcode this to "password" as value) These parameters are sent as form encoded url (Ex. username=[username]&password=[userpass]&grant_type=password). After successfull login, an object is returned to the client, containing the following properties: 1. access_token (the token itself) 2. token_type (type of the token, in this case "bearer") 3. expires_in (expiration time in seconds) On every following request to the API the client must include the access_token and its token_type within the request's header in the following format: "Authorization: token_type token" Example: Authorization: Bearer 1A2B3DgTy4f5g6h In case of questions or problems, please do not hesitate to contact us on the link below.

200.description: OK. Enum values: 0 = Normal User, 1 = Has OEI User, 2 = Should create OEI User

get.description: Returns list of clocked hours in specified date range. Max batch size is 100 records.

parameters.name: lastUpdatedOn
parameters.description: returns clocked hours updated after this value

post.description:
Manager is clocking out employees
                     Method: 0-ViewerFingerprint,
                             1-Android,
                             2-iOS,
                             3-ViewerPIN,
                             4-ViewerByManager,
                             5-BackOffice,
                             6-AndroidByManager,
                             7-iOSByManager

Values for ShiftId and ShiftStartDateTime or TeamId is/are mandatory. If the user wants to clock in through mobile apps (Method = Android (1) or iOS (2)), Coordinate object is mandatory to be send.
                     Method: 0-ViewerFingerprint,
                             1-Android,
                             2-iOS,
                             3-ViewerPIN,
                             4-ViewerByManager,
                             5-BackOffice,
                             6-AndroidByManager,
                             7-iOSByManager

If the user wants to clock out himselft through mobile apps (Method = Android (1) or iOS (2)), Coordinate object is not mandatory to be send anymore (HRK-16671).
                     Method: 0-ViewerFingerprint,
                             1-Android,
                             2-iOS,
                             3-ViewerPIN,
                             4-ViewerByManager,
                             5-BackOffice,
                             6-AndroidByManager,
                             7-iOSByManager
